[{"email_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0003", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "matthew.li@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Draft Acme Cloud change-order proposal for partner review", "content": "Hi George,

I reviewed the two proposed scope lines and agree they are clean expansions of the FY2026 outsourced-services grid under the existing Acme Cloud engagement. I do not see any audit-side independence concerns with adding the multi-state sales tax work for TX/GA/NC or the AR-aging bucket cleanup as drafted.

Once Andrea's revisions are incorporated into the addendum language and the client countersignature is back, I am comfortable countersigning Engagement Letter Addendum #1 on the firm side.

This should give you the approval chain you need before filing.

Best,
Matthew", "cc": ["andrea.phil@brookfieldcpas.com"], "parent_id": "email_scen_065_change_order_acme_0001", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-05-15T19:48:00Z", "is_read": true}, {"email_id": "ad391950add941e1bdd66f3754e64a55", "folder": "INBOX", "sender": "nia.okafor@brookfieldcpas.com", "recipients": ["george.mcadam@brookfieldcpas.com", "daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com", "sean.williams@brookfieldcpas.com"], "subject": "Re: Mon 3/2 onboarding: assign owners and flag document blockers", "content": "Hi George,

Following up on Monday's kickoff, I agree with the split between banking items and general support documents. On the bookkeeping side, I am blocked until I receive the prior balance sheet support, chart of accounts history, and any sales tax or payroll registrations. Once those are in hand, I can finish my review and confirm what is ready to go out versus what still needs client follow-up.

Best,
Nia", "cc": [], "parent_id": "dc9fe1ec878c41a58dc73ba627ad9003", "attachments": {}, "timestamp": "2026-03-02T16:01:29Z", "is_read": true}]

Updated TX liability is $24,492.10.\\n\\nRevised Q1 sales tax liability by state (SaaS-taxable only):\\nTX: $24,492.10\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt; no standing entry this period)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nGrand Q1 liability is $40,364.55.\\n\\nPer Hannah's correction, there is no CA standing accrual carried in from prior months, so the full $40,364.55 hits the quarter-end entry as a new incremental accrual.\\n\\nI'll carry the accrual on that basis and keep the TX nexus memo plus the SaaS-taxability note in the support file.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_2\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655120.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"You should treat TX as active for Q1. Acme crossed $612K trailing-12 Texas sales, so economic nexus was already met. That means TX is not $0 and you need destination-based local calculation by ZIP, not a single blended state rate.\\n\\nI checked the sales rollforward before replying. Please rerun TX on the per-ZIP local rates and update the thread total from there.\\n\\nOne more correction on the prior breakdown: GA, NC, CA, FL, and IL should all be $0 for Acme. Their SaaS revenue is not subject to sales tax in those states (services exempt or SaaS specifically carved out), so no filings are required this quarter. The old CA-only standing monthly entries were reclassified after the prior-period review, so there is no $29K sitting in 225000 from earlier months. Treat the full Q1 liability as a new incremental accrual.\", \"user\": \"persona_004\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_1\"}, {\"ts\": \"1775655000.000000\", \"type\": \"message\", \"text\": \"Kicking off Acme Q1 2026 sales tax cycle. Acme is pure SaaS, so I am only carrying liability in states where SaaS is taxable.\\n\\nCurrent state breakdown from prep file:\\nNY: $8,944.25\\nWA: $2,118.20\\nAZ: $4,810.00\\nTX: $0.00 pending nexus check\\nCA: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nFL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nIL: $0.00 (SaaS exempt)\\nMA: $0.00\\nNV: $0.00\\n\\nSubtotal before TX update: $15,872.45\\n\\nFlagging one open item before I finalize the accrual schedule: I am not fully comfortable with TX nexus treatment. Acme's trailing-12 TX sales look close to the threshold, and I want a second look on whether we stay origin-based/no filing versus move to local per-ZIP sourcing.\\n\\nIf TX nexus is triggered, I'll rerun the state using local rates and update the quarter total here.\", \"user\": \"persona_027\", \"reply_count\": 3, \"reply_users_count\": 2, \"latest_reply\": \"1777389480.000000\", \"client_msg_id\": \"scenario_scen_067_acme_multi_state_sales_tax_compound_cycle_kickoff_thread_0\"}], \"has_more\": false}"}

content.text:
The PA picture is now clear. From the April thread (channel C006):
- npc_025 (reviewer): "Brookfield crossed the $100K economic-nexus threshold in March, so April is the first filing month under the new threshold."
- npc_019 (preparer): "I already have PA on the post-threshold schedule at **6% on taxable services**. The PA tax comes out to **$3,950**."

So PA = 6% on PA taxable services. The April packet doc is a stub (no retrievable version/content). Let me now pull the actual GL data for May. Let me find the sales-tax liability accounts and the May period.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/may_packet.txt <<'EOF'
BROOKFIELD CPAs & ADVISORS - OWN-BOOKS SALES & USE TAX
May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet
STATUS: DRAFT - STAGED FOR REVIEW (Sofia Halabi) AND PARTNER APPROVAL (Ming Chang). DO NOT FILE BEFORE SIGN-OFF.

Entity:            Brookfield CPAs & Advisors (own books)
Period:            brookfield_FP-2026-05 (May 2026)
Jurisdictions:     New York, New Jersey, Connecticut, Pennsylvania
Prepared by:       Hannah Grant, Corporate Tax Senior - 2026-06-12
Reviewer:          Sofia Halabi
Approver:          Ming Chang (Partner) - sign-off required before any return is filed
Filing deadlines:  NY / NJ / CT due 2026-06-22 ; PA due 2026-06-25
Companion record:  "Brookfield April 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet"
                   (Records Vault doc_1af8dfb919a9444d) and reclass
                   JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-04-0071 ($27,400, approved by Ming Chang).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
1. WHY THIS IS MOVING NOW
------------------------------------------------------------------------
The monthly "Open May 2026 sales tax filing cycle" reminder fired 2026-06-01 and was
not actioned, so the May cycle had not been started. Confirmed in Oracle GL that no May
sales-tax accrual or reclass exists in brookfield_FP-2026-05 (the only sales-tax entry in
the prior period is the April reclass noted above). This packet opens and stages the cycle
so review and approval can happen well ahead of the late-June deadlines rather than as a
last-minute scramble.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
2. METHODOLOGY (consistent with the April cycle)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
- Base: taxable-services bookings by state (the firm's standard taxable-services cut).
  Note: this base is NOT the GL service-line revenue balance - April advisory/consulting
  revenue posted to 403000 was ~$13.4K, far below the implied NY taxable base, confirming
  the sales-tax base is sourced from the separate bookings cut.
- Rates: NY / NJ / CT at established state + local rates.
- PA: 6% on taxable services per the post-economic-nexus schedule adopted in April.
  Brookfield crossed PA's $100K economic-nexus threshold in March 2026; April 2026 was the
  first filing month under that threshold (per tax-channel thread, reviewer flag by the
  April reviewer and preparer confirmation of the 6% post-threshold schedule).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
3. MAY LIABILITY BY STATE  (PROVISIONAL - pending the May taxable-services cut)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
The per-state taxable-services-by-state cut for May has not yet been posted. Pending that
cut, amounts below are STAGED on the trailing-three-month / April basis directed by the
standing reminder ("track NJ, CT, PA on trailing average; keep PA on the post-economic-
nexus rate"). These are provisional staging figures to open the cycle, NOT final numbers.

   State   Basis / rate                         Provisional May amount
   -----   ----------------------------------   ----------------------
   NY      Taxable consulting/advisory bookings  ~$12,400  (confirm vs May bookings;
           x NY state+local rate                  April was elevated by two advisory
                                                   engagements that went live mid-April
                                                   and run the full month in May)
   NJ      Taxable services x NJ rate            included in ~$11,050 NJ+CT (split at cut)
   CT      Taxable services x CT rate            included in ~$11,050 NJ+CT (split at cut)
   PA      Taxable services x 6% (post-nexus)    ~$3,950
   -----                                         ----------------------
   TOTAL provisional                             ~$27,400

ACTION before approval: drop the May taxable-services-by-state cut, replace the provisional
figures with actuals, and confirm the NY movement and the NJ/CT split.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. PENNSYLVANIA - FLAGGED (not assumed through)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
PA is newly filed (first post-nexus month was April). For May it is STAGED at 6% on taxable
services for consistency with April, but the following are open and should be confirmed by
the reviewer/approver before filing - I am flagging rather than assuming:

  (a) No durable PA working paper exists. The April packet record in the vault is a
      98-byte stub with no retrievable content and no chain-of-custody, so the PA rate
      basis and taxable-base definition are not documented anywhere durable.
  (b) The flat 6% assumes NO PA local surtax. PA adds local tax in two jurisdictions:
      Philadelphia +2% (8% combined) and Allegheny County / Pittsburgh +1% (7% combined).
      If any PA taxable services are sourced to Philadelphia or Allegheny, 6% understates
      the liability. PA sourcing of the taxable services should be confirmed (and, if it
      affects PA, the April filing may warrant a look-back).
  (c) Confirm the taxable-base definition (which service lines are treated as PA-taxable).

  Recommendation: Ming / Sofia to confirm the PA rate basis and sourcing before filing.
  May PA is staged at 6% pending that confirmation.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. NEXT STEPS BEFORE FILING
------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1. Post the May taxable-services-by-state cut; finalize per-state amounts.
  2. Resolve the PA open items in section 4.
  3. Sofia Halabi review -> Ming Chang approval.
  4. On approval: book the May reclass into 230000 (mirroring the April JE), schedule
     payments, and file NY/NJ/CT by 2026-06-22 and PA by 2026-06-25.
EOF
echo "--- chars ---"; wc -c /tmp/may_packet.txt
echo "--- base64 (single line) ---"; base64 -w0 /tmp/may_packet.txt

input.subject: Approval needed: Brookfield May 2026 sales-tax packet (NY/NJ/CT due 6/22, PA 6/25)
input.content:
Hi Ming,

The May sales-tax cycle for our own books was missed at kickoff (the monthly nudge fired June 1 and wasn't picked up), so I've now opened and staged it. We're ahead of the deadlines, not behind them — but I want your sign-off before anything is filed, since the approval on these is yours.

What's done:
- Staged the May cycle for NY, NJ, CT, and PA and written it up alongside April's record in Records Vault: "Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet" (doc_eb9ae78648ea), companion to the April packet (doc_1af8dfb919a9444d) and the April reclass JE-brookfield-brookfield_FP-2026-04-0071 ($27,400, your approval).
- Confirmed in Oracle GL that no May sales-tax accrual/reclass exists yet in brookfield_FP-2026-05, so this genuinely starts the cycle.

Methodology is consistent with April: NY/NJ/CT at established rates; PA at 6% on taxable services per the post-economic-nexus schedule we adopted in April (we crossed PA's $100K threshold in March; April was month one). Per-state amounts in the packet are PROVISIONAL — staged on the trailing/April basis pending the May taxable-services-by-state cut, which needs to be dropped in and finalized before you approve.

PA flag (raising rather than assuming through):
- The April PA position isn't documented in a durable working paper (the April vault record is a 98-byte stub with no retrievable content).
- The flat 6% assumes no PA local surtax — Philadelphia +2% (8%) and Allegheny/Pittsburgh +1% (7%). If any PA taxable services source to those jurisdictions, 6% understates PA (and the April filing may warrant a look-back).
I've staged PA at 6% for consistency but would like you/Sofia to confirm the PA rate basis and sourcing before filing.

Next steps: Sofia to review and post the May taxable-services cut to firm up the per-state numbers, then your approval. Deadlines are NY/NJ/CT June 22 and PA June 25. I've also set myself a dated reminder so these don't slip.

Thanks,
Hannah

content.text:
The May cycle is now moving and staged. Here's where it stands:

## What I did

**Confirmed nothing had started.** The monthly "Open May 2026 sales tax filing cycle" reminder fired June 1 and was never actioned. Oracle GL confirms no May sales-tax accrual or reclass exists in `brookfield_FP-2026-05` — so this genuinely opens the cycle. Returns: **NY/NJ/CT due June 22, PA due June 25** (own books).

**Worked out what we owe (provisional).** The sales-tax base is the firm's *taxable-services-by-state bookings cut*, not GL revenue (April advisory revenue posted was only ~$13.4K, far below the implied NY tax base — so the base lives outside the ledger and hasn't been dropped for May yet). Staged on the April/trailing basis the standing reminder directs:

| State | Basis / rate | Provisional May |
|---|---|---|
| NY | taxable bookings × NY rate | ~$12,400 |
| NJ + CT | taxable services × state rates | ~$11,050 (split at the cut) |
| PA | taxable services × **6%** (post-nexus) | ~$3,950 |
| **Total** | | **~$27,400** |

These are staging figures to open the cycle; final per-state numbers get locked once the May taxable-services cut is posted, before Ming approves.

**Pennsylvania — flagged, not assumed through.** PA is kept at 6% on taxable services for consistency with April (we crossed the $100K economic-nexus threshold in March; April was month one). But I raised three open items rather than ruling them through:
- No durable PA working paper exists — the April vault record is a 98-byte stub with no retrievable content.
- The flat 6% assumes **no PA local surtax** (Philadelphia +2% → 8%, Allegheny/Pittsburgh +1% → 7%). If any PA taxable services source to those jurisdictions, 6% understates PA, and April may warrant a look-back.
- The taxable-base definition for PA should be confirmed.

## Staged and routed
1. **Write-up in Records Vault** alongside April: *"Brookfield May 2026 Multi-Jurisdiction Sales Tax Filing Packet"* (`doc_eb9ae78648ea`), companion to April's `doc_1af8dfb919a9444d`.
2. **Routed to Ming Chang for approval** (email, cc Sofia Halabi as reviewer per the April workflow) — explicitly: nothing filed before his sign-off.
3. **Tax-team note** posted to #tax-prep-and-filings so everyone knows May is moving.
4. **Dated reminder** set for June 18 covering the June 22 / June 25 deadlines, with the PA confirmation and approval gates spelled out.

One note: `brookfield_FP-2026-05` still shows status "open" in the GL despite its BD3/BD5 dates having passed — worth a glance during close, but it doesn't block staging the cycle.
